title: Territorians in path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle told to take cover in bathrooms as storm intensifies
summary_full: Very destructive winds gusting up to 195km/h are forecasted, with major flooding expected in Katherine by MondayGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAn emergency warning has been issued and thousands are bracing for Tropical Cyclone Narelle before its landfall in the Northern Territory, with winds of up to 195km/h expected.The highest-level warning had been issued around midday on Saturday and extends to Nhulunbuy to Port MacArthur, including Borroloola, Numbulwar, Alyangula and Gapuwiyak, the NT fire and emergency services commissioner, Andrew Wharton, said.
